channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

item.title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
item.description: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."

item.title: Mapping the path to clinical implementation of multi-omics
item.description: This Perspective maps the challenges facing clinical implementation of multi-omics, and outlines mitigation strategies involving pre-analytical, computational, regulatory and ethical frameworks, to accelerate its integration into routine healthcare.

item.title: Diving headfirst into the google newly released ‘ai governance in america’ framework
item.description: Google has released their proposed framework on AI governance in America. I unpack it. An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: Financial services’ AI dangers highlighted by regulator’s review
item.description: Britain’s financial regulator has been urged to consider regulation of large language models such as ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini because of their growing influence on consumer financial decisions. In a review commissioned by the Financial Conduct Authority and…

item.title: B2c is the new b2b; the reddit vs. geo challenge
item.description: Business Casual Is B2B marketing becoming a lot more, well, B2C? One big takeaway from Cannes Lions this year was that creators and influencers have taken over as the new celebs at the conference. Creator-style marketing has also taken over B2B tech company m…

item.title: Bispecific 10e8.4/imab broadly neutralizing antibody in people with or without hiv-1: a partially randomized phase 1 trial
item.description: A first-in-human, partially randomized and placebo-controlled phase I trial examined the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics and antiviral activity of the bispecific 10E8.4/iMab broadly neutralizing monoclonal antibody in people living with or without HIV,…

item.title: A new fda-approved oral weight loss pill requires no fasting: what makes foundayo different from other glp-1 drugs
item.description: FDA approved Foundayo (orforglipron) on April 1, 2026 — the only oral GLP-1 pill for weight loss you can take any time of day without fasting. Average weight loss: 12.4%. Here's the breakdown.

item.title: The FDA just approved the first inhaled mealtime insulin for children
item.description: FDA approved Afrezza inhaled insulin for children ages 6+ in May 2026. No needle required at mealtimes. Here's what INHALE-1 found and what families should know.

item.title: Broadcom and apple extend custom silicon pact to 2031
item.description: Probably an extension of long-running iThing innards arrangement, not a new custom AI accelerator deal

item.title: Network-based machine learning to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus
item.description: Researchers applied network-based machine learning methods to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). This approach integrates computational analysis with protein interaction networks to discover disease-specific molecular signatures. The study leverages collaborative expertise a

item.title: Circulating inflammatory, redox, and apoptosis-related alterations in drug-naive idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis: an exploratory case-control study
item.description: This exploratory case-control study examined circulating biomarkers related to inflammation, oxidative stress, and apoptosis in treatment-naive patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis compared to controls. The research identified specific alterations in these three pathological pathways that may

item.title: Targeted protein degradation: bridging chemical biology and clinical translation
item.description: This review examines targeted protein degradation technologies and their translation from chemical biology research into clinical applications, addressing the bridge between laboratory discovery and therapeutic development in this emerging field.

item.title: STAT+: FDA approves Vera Therapeutics kidney disease treatment
item.description: The FDA approved a new medicine from the biotech company Vera Therapeutics for patients with a type of chronic autoimmune kidney disease.

item.title: Run AI workloads on any cloud, store on Hugging Face: zero-egress storage with SkyPilot
item.description: Read the full article at the source.

item.title: Real-world comparative outcomes of darolutamide- versus abiraterone-based triplet therapy for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer: a retrospective cohort study
item.description: This retrospective cohort study compared real-world clinical outcomes between darolutamide- and abiraterone-based triplet therapy regimens in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. The analysis evaluated the effectiveness and safety of these two androgen receptor inhibitor appro

item.title: STAT+: I spoke to Anthropic’s CEO about how AI may affect biotech. Here’s what I learned
item.description: Call AI in biotech hype at your peril. There are real reasons that pharmaceutical companies are embracing this technology right now.

item.title: STAT+: GOP lawmakers push Trump’s FDA to make sure clinical trials are diverse
item.description: Supporters say clinical trial diversity is about making sure drugs and devices work, not about DEI.

item.title: Novartis buys UK biotech myricx for up to $1.5bn to chase a new cancer-drug class
item.description: Novartis has agreed to buy Myricx Bio, a small British oncology company, for up to $1.5bn, adding an early-stage but potentially significant technology to a cancer pipeline the industry keeps racing to expand. The Swiss group will pay $1.1bn upfront, with up …

item.title: “abolish the nih”? dr. scott atlas gives the antiscience game away
item.description: Dr. Scott Atlas, a senior fellow at the right wing think tank Hoover Institution who came to prominence as a COVID contrarian, recently wrote an op-ed advocating the elimination of the National Institutes of health and leaving the funding biomedical research …

item.title: Using human 3d organoid models to gain mechanistic insight in motor neuron diseases
item.description: Motor neuron disorders involve complex interactions between multiple CNS cell types, which are challenging to model in 2D in vitro conditions. Frizzi and colleagues describe recent advances in induced pluripotent stem cell-based 3D (organoid) models of the sp…

item.title: Papastergiou: greece sets out AI rules under EU framework
item.description: Greece will set out its legal and institutional framework for the operation and applications of artificial intelligence this week through draft legislation submitted to parliament, Digital Governance Minister Dimitris Papastergiou… Papastergiou: Greece sets o…

item.title: Genetic analysis of imaging-derived phenotypes
item.description: When integrated with genetic data, imaging-derived phenotypes (IDPs) enable large-scale analyses of genotype–phenotype relationships. Bian and Akey review analytical approaches to derive IDPs, key biological insights from genetic studies of imaging traits and…

item.title: Meta’s woes deepen in india as child abuse ads on instagram draw government ire
item.description: U.S. social media giant Meta is facing an onslaught of regulatory scrutiny in India, which has the largest user base for Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp.

item.title: Tracking AI in the supply chain
item.description: Supply chains have become a key testing ground for AI as businesses manage vast data volumes and complex networks

item.title: Fibronectin-induced overactivation of αvβ3-pi3k-pip3-pdk1-ilk signaling drives aortic disease in marfan syndrome
item.description: Here, the authors identify a fibronectin-driven signaling pathway linking extracellular matrix changes to aortic disease in Marfan syndrome. Targeting this pathway prevents and reverses aortic damage in models, suggesting new therapeutic strategies.

item.title: Antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates: design principles, intracellular delivery, and translational opportunities
item.description: This review examines antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates, focusing on their design principles, mechanisms for achieving intracellular delivery, and potential clinical applications. The work addresses key considerations for developing these hybrid therapeutics that combine the targeting specificity o

item.title: FLOWR.ROOT - A flow matching-based foundation model for joint multi-purpose structure-aware 3D ligand generation and affinity prediction
item.description: FLOWR.ROOT is a flow matching-based foundation model that enables joint multi-purpose 3D ligand generation and binding affinity prediction while incorporating structural information about target proteins. The model, developed by researchers at Pfizer's Machine Learning & Computational Sciences and C

item.title: STAT+: Four major biotech updates to catch up on
item.description: Anthropic CEO talks drug discovery, Medicare proposes steep 340B drug payment cuts, and more biotech news

item.title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
item.description: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.

item.title: How open models are driving AI research
item.description: Every year, the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) reveals where thousands of AI researchers have decided to put their work. This year’s accepted papers reveal a clear direction: open frontier models and open AI infrastructure have become fo…

item.title: Transcript: mamoon hamid, kleiner perkins
item.description: The transcript from this week’s, MiB: Mamoon Hamid, Kleiner Perkins on AI Investing, is below. You can stream and download our full conversation, including any podcast extras, on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, YouTube (video), YouTube (audio), and Bloomberg. All of…

item.title: Personalized, automated, standardized: inside vietnam's new surgical network
item.description: Vinmec's High-Tech Robotic Surgery Center Network combines AI, robotic platforms and standardized clinical protocols to deliver personalized surgical care while expanding training and remote treatment capabilities across Vietnam.

item.title: Illinois drops the hammer on AI companies
item.description: Pritzker isn't playing.

item.title: Data scientists are becoming AI managers, not model builders
item.description: The role is shifting from building models to managing them.

item.title: A senior fca official says britain should weigh regulating AI models directly
item.description: Britain should consider whether large language models such as ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini ought to be regulated as they increasingly influence how consumers make financial decisions, a senior official at the Financial Conduct Authority has said. Sheldon Mills…

item.title: Diagnostic performance of machine learning models based on dual-phase 99mtc-mibi spect/ct semiquantitative parameters for differentiating benign and malignant pulmonary nodules
item.description: Purpose To evaluate the diagnostic value of machine learning models based on dual-phase 99mTc-MIBI SPECT/CT semiquantitative parameters for differentiating benign and malignant pulmonary nodules. Methods This retrospective study included 132 patients with p…

item.title: Top 10 medtech deals in the first half of 2026
item.description: Boston Scientific, Medtronic and Stryker were among the companies to ink deals to move into markets that are expanding quickly.

item.title: Artificial intelligence-detected her2 strong-positive tumor proportion predicts fish positivity and treatment response in breast cancer
item.description: Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2)-targeted therapies have revolutionized breast cancer treatment, necessitating standardized HER2 testing. However, current immunochemistry-based HER2 assessment faces challenges due to subjectivity and variabilit…

item.title: AI health assistant app development: complete guide 2026
item.description: Build a next-gen AI health assistant app in 2026 with advanced features, real-world use cases, and cost insights for scalable healthcare innovation.

item.title: Reimagining data modeling on the lakehouse: introducing vibe data modeling
item.description: * Vibe Data Modeling is now available: a Databricks-native, LLM-powered agent that produces the analytical Silver-layer business model directly from a plain-English description of your business. * From prompt to deployed model in hours, replacing the six-to-…

item.title: Salesforce health cloud transforming medical data management
item.description: A guide to Salesforce Health Cloud for life sciences: features, the FHIR data model, AI with Agentforce and Einstein, EHR integration, and HIPAA compliance

item.title: Extracting host-specific developmental signatures from longitudinal microbiome data
item.description: Author summary Longitudinal microbiome datasets are complex, consisting of repeated high-dimensional compositions that track changes in microbial abundance across individuals over time. While tensor decompositions are powerful tools for unraveling structure i…

item.title: Agreement between ems provider-assigned prehospital triage and initial emergency department triage in pediatric and adult ems-transported encounters: a retrospective observational study
item.description: Background Prehospital triage supports destination decisions, resource activation, and early risk stratification, yet accurate triage of children in the field remains challenging. In South Korea, a five-level emergency department triage framework has recently…

item.title: Key targets and mechanisms by which gut microbiota-derived metabolites regulate alzheimer’s disease through the immune - inflammatory pathway: based on network pharmacology and molecular docking
item.description: This study integrated network pharmacology, bioinformatics, and molecular docking to explore potential immune-inflammatory pathways associated with the relationship between gut microbiota-derived metabolites and Alzheimer’s disease (AD). A total of 260 gut mi…

item.title: Chagas disease in northern minas gerais: clinical and epidemiological features of patients at a pioneering specialized outpatient service
item.description: Author summary Chagas Disease remains a significant public health problem in northern Minas Gerais, Brazil, a region where access to specialized healthcare services is still limited. To address this gap, a specialized Chagas Disease outpatient clinic was rece…

item.title: Completion of rabies post-exposure prophylaxis in ouagadougou, burkina faso, 2021–2023: a cross-sectional analysis of routine data
item.description: Author summary Rabies is a preventable but almost invariably fatal disease once symptoms appear. Post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP), consisting of timely rabies vaccination after a suspected exposure, is highly effective when completed according to recommendatio…

item.title: Genetics of hypertension in populations affected by the aral sea ecological crisis: a case-control study
item.description: Objective This research investigates the correlation between nine polymorphic variants and the incidence of hypertension and its clinical manifestations in the Ellikqala District in the Republic of Karakalpakstan, Uzbekistan. This district is known as one of …

item.title: Perceptions and public health risks of the bat-human interface in households from fragmented rural landscapes in southern chile
item.description: Land-use change and habitat fragmentation in southern Chile have favored synanthropic bat species, promoting their contact with domestic animals and humans, representing a potential risk of exposure. This study aimed to characterize dwellings with bat colonie…

item.title: The 'first' ai-run ransomware attack still needed a human | techcrunch
item.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully aut…
